Description: Verify user can view their bookshelf with standard lists (Favourites; Reading; Wishlist)
Preconditions: User is logged in; user has books in at least one list
Test Steps: 1. Login as user 2. Navigate to /bookshelf 3. Observe the Favourites; Reading and Wishlist sections
Test Data: userId: logged-in user with books in bookshelf
Expected Output: All three standard lists displayed with correct book counts and cover images
Actual Output: Bookshelf page loaded and displayed Favourites; Reading and Wishlist sections each showing the correct number of books with cover images and titles

Test Title: Add Book to Favourites from Books Page
Description: Verify user can add a book to the Favourites list directly from the Books catalogue page
Preconditions: User is logged in; at least one book exists in the catalogue
Test Steps: 1. Login as user 2. Navigate to /books 3. Click on a book card 4. Click Add to Bookshelf 5. Select Favourites from the dropdown
Test Data: Book: Clean Code, Target List: favourites
Expected Output: Book added to Favourites; success toast message displayed; book appears in Bookshelf under Favourites
Actual Output: Book Clean Code was added to Favourites successfully; a success toast notification appeared and the book was visible in the Bookshelf under the Favourites section

Test Title: Add Book to Wishlist from Books Page
Description: Verify user can add a book to the Wishlist via the dropdown on the book listing page
Preconditions: User is logged in; book exists in catalogue
Test Steps: 1. Login as user 2. Navigate to /books 3. Click on a book 4. Click Add to Bookshelf 5. Select Wishlist from the dropdown
Test Data: Book: Atomic Habits, Target List: wishlist
Expected Output: Book added to Wishlist; success toast shown; book visible in Bookshelf Wishlist section
Actual Output: Book Atomic Habits was added to the Wishlist; a success confirmation toast appeared and the book appeared in the Bookshelf Wishlist section on next visit

Description: Verify user can remove a book from a bookshelf list using the remove button on the book card
Preconditions: User is logged in; at least one book exists in any bookshelf list
Test Steps: 1. Login as user 2. Navigate to /bookshelf 3. Hover over a book card 4. Click the X (Remove) button 5. Confirm removal
Test Data: Book in Favourites list; bookId: existing entry
Expected Output: Book removed from the list; book count decremented; toast confirmation shown
Actual Output: Book was removed from the Favourites list; the card disappeared immediately; the book count updated and a success toast message appeared confirming removal

Description: Verify user can move a book from one list to another using the Move button
Preconditions: User is logged in; at least one book exists in the Wishlist
Test Steps: 1. Login as user 2. Navigate to /bookshelf 3. Hover over a book in Wishlist 4. Click Move button 5. Select Reading from the move modal
Test Data: Book in Wishlist; target: reading list
Expected Output: Book removed from Wishlist and added to Reading; both lists update immediately
Actual Output: Book was successfully moved from Wishlist to the Reading list; it disappeared from Wishlist and appeared in the Reading section with a toast confirmation message

Description: Verify user can create a new custom bookshelf list to organise books
Preconditions: User is logged in; Bookshelf page is accessible
Test Steps: 1. Login as user 2. Navigate to /bookshelf 3. Click Create New List button 4. Enter a custom list name 5. Click Create
Test Data: Custom list name: Must Read Classics
Expected Output: New custom list created and visible in the bookshelf alongside standard lists; empty state shown
Actual Output: Custom list Must Read Classics was created successfully; it appeared in the bookshelf navigation and showed an empty state prompting the user to add books

Test Title: Clear All Books from a Bookshelf List
Description: Verify user can clear all books from a list using the Clear All action
Preconditions: User is logged in; target list has at least two books
Test Steps: 1. Login as user 2. Navigate to /bookshelf 3. Open a list section 4. Click Clear All 5. Confirm the action in the modal
Expected Output: All books removed from the list; list shows empty state; toast confirmation displayed
Actual Output: All three books were removed from the Wishlist after confirming the Clear All action; the section showed the empty state message and a success toast appeared

Description: Verify user can permanently delete a custom bookshelf list they created
Preconditions: User is logged in; at least one custom list exists
Test Steps: 1. Login as user 2. Navigate to /bookshelf 3. Open a custom list section 4. Click Delete List button 5. Confirm deletion
Expected Output: Custom list deleted and removed from navigation; books removed; confirmation toast shown
Actual Output: Custom list Must Read Classics was deleted successfully; it was removed from the bookshelf navigation and all books within it were cleared; a toast confirmation appeared

Description: Verify the system shows an appropriate error when a user tries to add a book already present in the same list
Preconditions: User is logged in; a book already exists in the Favourites list
Test Steps: 1. Login as user 2. Navigate to /books 3. Select a book already in Favourites 4. Click Add to Bookshelf 5. Select Favourites
Test Data: Book already in Favourites: Clean Code
Expected Output: Error or warning toast shown indicating the book is already in the selected list; no duplicate created
Actual Output: The system displayed a warning toast indicating the book Clean Code had already been added or the borrow limit was reached; no duplicate entry was created in the Favourites list

Description: Verify an appropriate empty state message is shown when a bookshelf list contains no books
Preconditions: User is logged in; a list (e.g. Reading) is empty
Test Steps: 1. Login as user 2. Navigate to /bookshelf 3. Select the Reading list tab 4. Observe the content area
Expected Output: Empty state UI shown with icon; message; and an Explore Library button
Actual Output: The Reading list displayed an empty state with an icon; a message saying the shelf is empty; and an Explore Library button that navigated to the books catalogue
